instructions:
Build a doc-as-test framework for TypeScript: extract every TS/JS code block from a curated set of high-traffic doc pages, compile each one with `tsc --noEmit --strict` against the real `@amityco/ts-sdk` source, emit a pass/fail report.

This is the trust-building layer beyond the regex validator. The regex validator catches stale API names; doc-as-test catches WRONG SIGNATURES, wrong arg types, wrong async/await patterns ŌĆö the class of errors that would have surfaced the 6 false FLAG_FOR_SDK triage decisions corrected in commit bca3e33d.

Scope: TypeScript only in v1. Cohort-balanced selection ŌĆö chat-track pages (Eastern customer hot path) + social-track pages (Western customer hot path) per usage analytics. ~24 pages, ~80% of customer code-path coverage.

## Part A ŌĆö Directory + tsconfig

Create `.docs-ops/integration-tests/` with this structure:

```
.docs-ops/integration-tests/
  README.md                  ŌåÉ brief: what this is, how to run locally
  preamble.d.ts              ŌåÉ shared `declare const` context vars
  pages.json                 ŌåÉ the 24 page paths (so the runner reloads without spec changes)
  tsconfig.json              ŌåÉ strict, noEmit, points at AmityTypescriptSDK source
  extract-blocks.py          ŌåÉ walks pages.json, emits one .ts per code block
  run-tests.py               ŌåÉ runs tsc per extracted .ts, emits results/latest.json
  results/
    latest.json              ŌåÉ run report
    extracted/               ŌåÉ generated .ts files (gitignore .ts but keep .gitkeep)
```

`tsconfig.json` must:
  - `strict: true`, `noEmit: true`, `target: 'es2020'`, `module: 'esnext'`, `moduleResolution: 'bundler'`
  - `paths`: map `@amityco/ts-sdk` to the local SDK source via `SP_SDKS_ROOT` (use the same env-var pattern as the TS extractor ŌĆö default to repo-parent sibling layout). Specifically: `"@amityco/ts-sdk": ["<SP_SDKS_ROOT>/AmityTypescriptSDK/packages/sdk/src/index.ts"]`.
  - Include `preamble.d.ts` + the extracted `.ts` files.
  - Skip lib check (the SDK source has many internal types we don't want to lint).

## Part B ŌĆö Preamble

Draft `preamble.d.ts` with `declare const` for context variables that docs assume exist in their snippets. Pull types from `.docs-ops/sdk-surface/typescript.json`. Minimum set (verify each type against the surface before declaring):

```typescript
// Setup
declare const apiKey: string;
declare const region: 'sg' | 'eu' | 'us';
declare const client: Amity.Client;
declare const userId: string;
declare const displayName: string;
declare const authToken: string;

// Chat-cohort
declare const channelId: string;
declare const subChannelId: string;
declare const messageId: string;
declare const channel: Amity.Channel;
declare const message: Amity.Message;

// Social-cohort
declare const postId: string;
declare const communityId: string;
declare const commentId: string;
declare const pollId: string;
declare const post: Amity.Post;
declare const comment: Amity.Comment;
declare const community: Amity.Community;

// File / media
declare const fileId: string;
declare const imageUrl: string;
```

Verify each `Amity.X` type exists in the surface (`typescript.json` -> `namespaces.Amity.members`). If any don't exist, drop them from the preamble and note in your report. Be CONSERVATIVE ŌĆö false declarations create false negatives (block passes that should pass).

This preamble WILL be wrong on the first iteration. That's expected. Iterate.

## Part C ŌĆö Page list (`pages.json`)

Write the 24 cohort-balanced pages. Verify each path exists; for the entries marked `enumerate:`, walk the subdirectory and include all `.mdx` files.

```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "selection_rationale": "Top-traffic pages per API usage analytics (cohort-balanced: chat-heavy Eastern + social-heavy Western customers).",
  "chat_track": [
    "social-plus-sdk/getting-started/platform-setup/web/web-quick-start.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/getting-started/authentication.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/conversation-management/channels/query-channels.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/conversation-management/channels/get-channel.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/conversation-management/channels/update-channel.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/conversation-management/members/join-leave-channel.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/conversation-management/members/query-members.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/messaging-features/messages/query-and-filter-messages.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/messaging-features/message-creation/send-a-message.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/chat/messaging-features/messages/flag-unflag-a-message.mdx"
  ],
  "social_track": [
    "social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/content-handling/files-images-and-videos/file.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/content-handling/files-images-and-videos/image-handling.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/content-handling/files-images-and-videos/video-handling.mdx",
    "enumerate:social-plus-sdk/social/content-management/comments/",
    "enumerate:social-plus-sdk/social/content-management/posts/moderation/",
    "enumerate:social-plus-sdk/social/content-management/posts/retrieval/",
    "social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/user-management/user-operations/update-user-information.mdx"
  ],
  "shared": [
    "social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/user-management/user-operations/get-user-information.mdx",
    "social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/realtime-communication/push-notifications/register-and-unregister-push-notifications-on-a-device.mdx",
    "enumerate:social-plus-sdk/core-concepts/realtime-communication/push-notifications/settings/"
  ]
}
```

Resolve `enumerate:` entries at extract time. Verify all final paths exist before extraction.

## Part D ŌĆö Block extractor (`extract-blocks.py`)

Walk `pages.json`. For each page, find every ```typescript / ```ts / ```javascript / ```js fenced block (mirror the FENCE_RE pattern from `ts-accuracy-validator.py`). For each block:
  - Write to `results/extracted/<page-slug>-block-<N>.ts`
  - Prepend a triple-slash reference to the preamble: `/// <reference path="../preamble.d.ts" />`
  - Then the block contents verbatim (NO modifications)
  - Preserve the block's own `import` statements as-is

Page slug: replace `/` with `__` and strip `.mdx`. So `social-plus-sdk/chat/conversation-management/channels/query-channels.mdx` -> `social-plus-sdk__chat__conversation-management__channels__query-channels-block-1.ts`.

Also handle indented code blocks inside MDX components (`<Step>`, `<Tab>`, `<Accordion>`) ŌĆö the FENCE_RE in the regex validator misses some of these; check using a relaxed pattern. Document in `extract-blocks.py` what variants you handle.

## Part E ŌĆö Test runner (`run-tests.py`)

For each `.ts` file in `results/extracted/`:
  - Run `tsc --noEmit --project .docs-ops/integration-tests/tsconfig.json` (or per-file: `tsc --noEmit --strict <file>` with the tsconfig as base)
  - Capture stdout + stderr + exit code
  - Pass = exit 0; Fail = non-zero
  - For fails: parse the first ~5 error lines for human-readable summary

Emit `results/latest.json`:

```json
{
  "run_at": "<iso>",
  "sdk_root": "<resolved SP_SDKS_ROOT/AmityTypescriptSDK>",
  "preamble_version": "<hash>",
  "stats": {
    "pages_scanned": <int>,
    "blocks_extracted": <int>,
    "blocks_passed": <int>,
    "blocks_failed": <int>,
    "pass_rate": <float>
  },
  "by_page": {
    "<page>": { "blocks": <int>, "passed": <int>, "failed": <int> }
  },
  "failures": [
    {
      "block_file": "results/extracted/<slug>-block-1.ts",
      "source_page": "<page>",
      "source_line_range": "<start>-<end>",
      "errors": ["TS2339: Property 'foo' does not exist on type 'AmityClient'.", "..."]
    }
  ]
}
```

Group failures by source page in the report ŌĆö easier triage. Include the source page's line range for the block so a human can jump to the right spot.

## Part F ŌĆö README + .gitignore + first run

`README.md` should be ~20 lines: what this is, how to run locally (`python3 run-tests.py`), how to read the report.

Add `results/extracted/*.ts` to `.gitignore` (generated artifacts).

RUN the framework end-to-end. Capture the first run's `latest.json`. Expect MANY failures ŌĆö typical first-run pass rate is 40-60% because the preamble is incomplete. Don't try to fix them in this task; report them in `notes` so the next round can refine.

## Out of scope for this task

- Wiring into `check-drift.py` as a gate ŌĆö that's a follow-up once the framework's false positive rate is acceptable (<10%).
- Other platforms (iOS / Android / Flutter) ŌĆö TS only for v1.
- Runtime tests ŌĆö type-check only.
- Fixing the failures ŌĆö just report them. Iterating on the preamble is a follow-up.

## Success criteria (LOOSE ŌĆö this is a framework build, not a quality gate yet)

- Framework runs end-to-end.
- `results/latest.json` is produced with valid schema.
- At least 50 TS blocks were extracted (sanity check that the pages were actually scanned).
- At least one block PASSES (sanity check that the toolchain works ŌĆö if zero pass, the tsconfig is wrong, not the docs).

Report in `notes`: stats from the first run; top 10 most common failure reasons (e.g., 'Cannot find name X' is a preamble gap; 'Type X is not assignable to Y' is a real signature error); estimate of what fraction of failures look like preamble gaps vs real doc errors; any pages where ALL blocks failed (likely points at preamble issues, not the docs).

validators.name: framework_runs_and_produces_valid_report
validators.command: test -f .docs-ops/integration-tests/results/latest.json && python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('.docs-ops/integration-tests/results/latest.json')); assert 'stats' in d and 'failures' in d; assert d['stats']['blocks_extracted']>=50, f'too few blocks: {d[\"stats\"][\"blocks_extracted\"]}'; assert d['stats']['blocks_passed']>=1, 'zero blocks passed ŌĆö toolchain likely broken'; print('OK')"
